items.title: Noted on pod but my broadest take on the Devin Carter trade is...
items.body_excerpt:
Noted on pod but my broadest take on the Devin Carter trade is...

If the Hawks had traded a future asset for Carter, I would've been "meh" on the deal, even while totally understanding it.

Instead, the Hawks RECEIVED a future asset to take Carter, pushing it to a "clear yes" for me.

items.title: Small CBA thing to watch for with Raptors trades:
items.body_excerpt:
Small CBA thing to watch for with Raptors trades:

They need to take back less salary than they send out, or else they're hard-capped at the 1st apron.

That's especially hard for them, as they have $6.6M in "unlikely" incentives that count for apron purposes (but not trade/cap).

items.title: Bonus Hollander POTD Bubbles Hawkins. Illinois St SG drafted 51st by GS in 75. Barely played & cut. Signed by NJ in 76-77 & had 19.3 ppg but fought w/coach & was cut in 77-78. NBA career was over after a 4 gm stint w/DET in 78-79. Struggled after & was sadly killed in 93. RIP.

The Raptors luxury tax/apron situation is fluid due to Kawhi Leonard's $7.45 million trade bonus.

He will need to waive most of it unless Raptors shed more payroll.

They'd have enough room to fill their final four roster spots with minimum salaries if he waives it all.

This likely closes the door on Sandro Mamukelashvili returning.

Per Cleaning the Glass, the Hawks had a 113.0 defensive rating with EITHER Onyeka Okongwu or Jock Landale at center last season.

That equates to the 6th-best defense in the NBA.

(Contrary to popular belief, they’ve proven they can defend at a high level without a dominant 7-foot rim protector!)

items.title: The NBA Moratorium has started. Outside of a few exceptions, NBA signings and trades can be agreed to, but not executed until July 6.
items.body_excerpt:
The NBA Moratorium has started. Outside of a few exceptions, NBA signings and trades can be agreed to, but not executed until July 6.

Some of the exceptions:

🏀Rookie signings via Rookie Scale or Second Round Pick Exception
🏀Veteran Minimum signing
🏀Two-Way signings

items.title: Free agent guard Marcus Smart has agreed on a two-year, $13 million deal with the Houston Rockets, plus a player option for the second season, sources tell ESPN. The former Defensive Player of the Year reunites with Ime Udoka for significant opportunity in the Rockets backcourt.
items.body_excerpt: Free agent guard Marcus Smart has agreed on a two-year, $13 million deal with the Houston Rockets, plus a player option for the second season, sources tell ESPN. The former Defensive Player of the Year reunites with Ime Udoka for significant opportunity in the Rockets backcourt.

Expect the Marcus Smart contract with the Rockets to come in just at or below the Taxpayer MLE amount of $6.06M. That way Houston avoids a first-apron hard cap and potential issues with matching offer sheets for Tari Eason.

Rockets can still shed salary in other trades too.

items.title: Further evidence that the Pistons acquiring John Collins will be a S&T.

Further evidence that the Pistons acquiring John Collins will be a S&T.

In a S&T, a contract must be for three years, but only the first season has to be fully guaranteed.

Pistons could tie this into the Isaiah Stewart to Memphis trade, or as a part of something bigger. TBD!

items.title: Explaining NBA "Mutual Options":
items.body_excerpt:
Explaining NBA "Mutual Options":

Not an actual CBA term, but a good descriptor for what's becoming a popular trend in the NBA. What happens is that on the final year of a contract, the player has an option. But if they opt in, the deal is then partially/non-guaranteed. Thus = mutual option.

items.title: My thoughts on what the Celtics signing Mike Conley could mean for a potential Anfernee Simons reunion:
items.body_excerpt:
My thoughts on what the Celtics signing Mike Conley could mean for a potential Anfernee Simons reunion:

One can't ignore financial side of roster building, or that teams must plan with more than one year in mind. However, to me, this largely depends on Payton Pritchard's role next season.

Story ⬇️

Michael Scott: Guess who just ordered from your favorite pizza place, Alfredo?
Kevin: Wait. Alfredo's pizza cafe? Or Pizza by Alfredo?

Kevin: Oscar, talk to him.
Oscar: Michael, there's a very big difference between these two pizza places. Both in quality of ingredients and in overall taste. Which one did you order from?
Michael Scott: Pizza by Alfredo. [everyone groans] All right, you know what? Okay, okay. What is better, a medium amount of good pizza, or all you can eat of pretty good pizza?
All: A medium amount of good pizza.
